timeline.label: Q3 FY2026 10-Q: nine-month revenue $44.9M; cash $24.5M (down from $49.2M); inventory $42.0M
424B5 prospectus supplement filed; equity raise capacity for working capital
8-K: management highlights silicon carbide wafer-level burn-in customer engagements
FY2025 10-K: revenue $65.0M (−2% YoY), operating income $13.4M (+33% YoY), OCF $10.0M; cash $49.2M
424B5 equity offering; raises growth capital during SiC adoption cycle
8-K: announces acquisition integration expanding test-system portfolio
424B5 equity offering during prior semicap up-cycle
424B5 equity offering; funds capacity expansion for burn-in systems
Aehr Test Systems founded; pioneers burn-in and test equipment for semiconductors

industry.structure: Semiconductor test and burn-in equipment is an oligopoly adjacent to front-end capital equipment: Teradyne, Advantest, and FormFactor dominate broad ATE; AEHR competes in niche wafer-level and burn-in systems with high customer qualification barriers.
industry.share_shift: AEHR gained attention as silicon carbide power-device makers adopt wafer-level burn-in; FY2025 revenue dipped 2% YoY while margins expanded, but Q3 FY2026 nine-month revenue trough signals order timing volatility.
industry.trend: EV and industrial SiC adoption drives wafer-level test demand; semicap spending cycles and customer concentration create boom-bust order patterns and working-capital swings.
